soil_types.name: Alluvial Soil
soil_types.characteristics: Highly fertile, rich in potash, poor in nitrogen and phosphorus

soil_types.name: Black Cotton Soil (Regur)
soil_types.characteristics: High water retention, rich in calcium and magnesium, poor in nitrogen and phosphorus

soil_types.name: Red and Yellow Soil
soil_types.characteristics: Porous, good drainage, low fertility, deficient in NPK, calcium and organic matter

soil_types.name: Laterite Soil
soil_types.characteristics: Poor in lime, nitrogen, phosphate, potash; rich in iron and aluminium

soil_types.name: Sandy Soil
soil_types.characteristics: Low water retention, low fertility, highly permeable, low organic matter
improvement: Add clay or compost to improve water retention

pH.acidic_correction: Apply agricultural lime or dolomite at 2–4 tonnes/ha based on pH. Lime neutralizes acidity; improves nutrient availability.
pH.alkaline_correction: Apply gypsum (calcium sulphate) at 2–5 tonnes/ha. Grow salt-tolerant crops. Improve drainage.
pH.testing_frequency: Once every 3 years or when crop health declines

nitrogen.deficiency_symptoms: Yellowing of older/lower leaves (chlorosis), stunted growth, pale green colour
nitrogen.correction: Apply urea (46% N) or ammonium sulphate (21% N). Use FYM or compost. Grow legume cover crops.
nitrogen.excess_symptoms: Dark green leaves, excessive vegetative growth, delayed maturity, increased pest susceptibility
nitrogen.testing: Soil Test Kit or laboratory analysis

phosphorus.deficiency_symptoms: Purple/reddish coloration of leaves, poor root development, delayed maturity
phosphorus.correction: Apply Single Super Phosphate (SSP) 16% P2O5 or DAP (46% P2O5). Use phosphate-solubilizing bacteria.
phosphorus.note: Apply near root zone as P is immobile in soil

potassium.deficiency_symptoms: Scorching or burning of leaf tips and margins (starting from older leaves), weak stems
potassium.correction: Apply Muriate of Potash (MOP 60% K2O) or Sulphate of Potash (SOP). Wood ash is an organic source.
potassium.note: Critical for disease resistance, water regulation, and fruit quality

organic_matter.low_om_correction: Apply FYM 10–15 tonnes/ha, vermicompost, green manuring with dhaincha/sunhemp, crop residue incorporation
organic_matter.benefits: Improves soil structure, water retention, microbial activity, nutrient availability

zinc.deficiency_symptoms: Khaira disease in rice, white bud in maize, little leaf in citrus
zinc.correction: Apply zinc sulphate 25 kg/ha or foliar spray of 0.5% zinc sulphate solution

boron.deficiency_symptoms: Hollow stem in cauliflower, cracked fruit in apple, poor fruit setting
boron.correction: Apply borax 10 kg/ha or foliar spray of 0.1–0.2% borax

iron.deficiency_symptoms: Interveinal chlorosis (yellowing between green veins) in young leaves
iron.correction: Apply ferrous sulphate 25 kg/ha or foliar spray of 0.5% FeSO4 with 0.1% citric acid

soil_testing.importance: Soil testing is the foundation of scientific farming. It prevents over/under-fertilization, saves cost, and improves yield.
soil_testing.government_initiative: Soil Health Card (SHC) – Government of India provides free soil testing and health card to farmers. Visit nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or Agriculture Department office.
soil_testing.testing_labs: Contact local Agriculture Department, KVK, ICAR institutes, or private labs. Kisan Call Centre: 1800-180-1551
